note: Per-question fact rubric for the reference-catalog eval. FAIRNESS RULE: every independent:true question grades ONLY a fact whose ground truth exists in the real Fowler/Kerievsky books (pattern MEMBERSHIP in a refactoring's row, a real 1st->2nd-ed rename/merge, a real smell provenance, a real print-vs-web provenance). NO direction word (away/toward/towards/de-pattern/dismantle/retreat) is ever a required or forbidden grade token. mustName.all = AND list (each must appear affirmed); mustName.anyOf = array of OR-groups (each group needs >=1 affirmed hit); mustNotClaim = forbidden claims (fail if any appears un-negated). Matching is word-bounded + whitespace-tolerant + clause-scoped negation-aware (see grade-reference.mjs).

targets.fact: Move Accumulation to Visitor sits in two pattern rows: it heads toward Visitor and steps back from Iterator. Iterator is the discriminator (base may name only the pattern in the title).
Encapsulate Composite with Builder heads toward Builder but steps back from Composite. Membership of both patterns is the real table fact.
The named Kerievsky move for an unwarranted Singleton is Inline Singleton; a plain functional alternative is a module namespace / module of functions.

Exactly three Kerievsky refactorings move away from a pattern: Inline Singleton, Encapsulate Composite with Builder, Move Accumulation to Visitor. Grade membership of the three real refactoring names.
Combinatorial Explosion is a Kerievsky-unique smell (Refactoring to Patterns Ch.4), not a Fowler smell.
Primitive Obsession is catalogued by both Fowler and Kerievsky.
Fowler generalized 1st-ed Introduce Null Object into 2nd-ed Introduce Special Case.
One 2nd-ed refactoring, Change Function Declaration, absorbed Add Parameter + Remove Parameter + Rename Method.
Return Modified Value is in Fowler's online catalog only, not in the printed 2nd edition.

anyOf: not in the second edition print

targets.fact: Fowler's 2nd ed has NO Extract Interface refactoring; the case maps to Change Function Declaration / Extract Superclass.

anyOf: does not include an Extract Interface
does not have an Extract Interface

targets.fact: The 1st-ed Consolidate Duplicate Conditional Fragments case is covered by 2nd-ed Slide Statements (a real 2nd-ed rename/merge).
Indecent Exposure is a Kerievsky-unique smell, not a Fowler smell and not absent from both.
Saturation control: base knows Extract Function cold. Loose match only; reported separately.
Saturation control: base knows the Strategy pattern cold. Loose match only; reported separately.
